highlights.text: It is also why I wrote last Wednesday at 4pm: “Bear Case Thursday: Begins on the failure of 6819”.

I wrote last Friday at 4pm: “My general lean is ES can try and recover 6689 to generate a rally to 6743, 6754, 6766, then 6819” We got started right on this after 6pm Sunday and prior to FOMC yesterday we got to 6815 or so (a few points shy) completing the back-test of last weeks breakdown.
Last Thursday morning, price picked its path, and down we went going elevator down .
Every elevator down sell sees an equally violent squeeze the other way.
Those squeezes however, only occur when price triggers my core setup: The Failed Breakdown which occurs when price flushes a major low, traps shorts, then reverses.
1) To start a squeeze, they’d need to put in a Failed Breakdown and to do this, theyd need to recover 6689 which was a big shelf all day last Friday.
In order to mount a recovery today, bulls would need to put in another Failed Breakdown.
The most obvious was the Failed Breakdown of the March 8th 6635 low - a monster low from which we rallied 200+ points.
I wrote yesterday at 4pm: “A much safer entry than just buying this is to wait for a Failed Breakdown of last weeks 6635 low.
In today’s newsletter I’ll talk this, I’ll go over today’s Failed Breakdowns (these are key to know), and I’ll discuss the actionable plan for tomorrow

post_title: Every Bounce Was Sold In SPX This Week. Will Today's Be Too? March 20 Plan
